title: ORGANIC OM MUSHROOM POWDER BLEND
body_html:
Ingredients: organic Chaga dual extract powder, organic reishi dual extract powder, organic cordyceps extract powder, organic Coriolus extract powder, organic lion's mane extract powder, organic maitake extract powder
Origin: China    Processed: Canada
Production: All mushroom have been cultivated in greenhouses with natural lighting and fresh air flow, and on substrate materials native to each particular mushroom in order to produce one of the highest quality and most potent mushroom extract powders available on the market. Hot water extraction (aka tea) is a very simple and effective way to concentrate and increase the bioavailability of all the important medicinal properties contained in mushrooms. The combined use of alcohol and water is crucial when some important compounds in the mushroom are not water soluble, such as triterpenoids in Reishi and Chaga.
Qualities: Each ingredient has its own beneficial properties:

  • Chaga (Inonotus obliquus) 8:1 - dual (hot water/alcohol) extract powder. Traditionally used as an immune modulator, to balance blood sugar, or to detoxify blood and liver during chemotherapy.
  • Reishi (Ganoderma lucidum) 16:1 - dual (hot water/alcohol) extract powder. Contain hundreds of bioactive compounds that may help balance the immune system and have anti-inflammatory, sleep-promoting, antibacterial and antiviral qualities.
  • Cordyceps (Cordyceps militaris) 1:1 - hot water extract powder. Cordyceps militaris has similar active compounds and activities to C. sinensis and is traditionally used for fatigue, general weakness, improved respiratory function and improved sexual function.
  • Coriolus (Trametes versicolor) 1:1 - hot water extract powder. Wild Trametes, also known as "turkey tail", grows naturally worldwide in all types of forests although it is primarily found on deciduous trees. Medicinally it has shown to be useful for all types of conditions where immunity is compromised.
  • Lion's Mane (Hericium erinaceus) 1:1 - hot water extract powder. Lion's Mane is a choice edible that grows wild on deciduous trees and is an immunomodulator that is useful for many conditions where immunity is compromised. It has also been used for gastritis. Current research suggests that it may have some effect on nerve growth.
  • Maitake (Grifola frondosa) 1:1 - hot water extract powder. Maitake is a choice edible that only recently has been utilized for its medicinal properties. During the 1990's extensive research demonstrated the activity of maitake beta-glucans as immune system potentiators.

Common Use: Add this restorative mix to smoothies, juices, coffee or teas, or a wide variety of recipes.
Storage: Store in a cool, dark, dry place in an airtight container. Refrigeration not required.
Safety: We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using any herbal products, particularly if you are pregnant, nursing, or on any medications.

title: ALMOND BUTTER, roasted, smooth
body_html:
Processed in: Canada  Grown in: Italy
Production: almonds are gently roasted and ground to a creamy, smooth paste.
Qualities: Almonds are a rich source of antioxidants and vitamins like B2, biotin, and E as well as many essential minerals such as calcium, iron, magnesium, manganese, potassium, phosphorus, copper, and zinc. They are also rich in dietary fiber and mono and polyunsaturated fats. Typical of many nuts and seeds, almonds contain phytosterols which have been associated with cholesterol-lowering properties. 
Results from a recent study [1] showed evidence that nut consumption (about 20 almonds) may reduce the risk of coronary heart disease, stroke, cardiovascular disease, total cancer, and all-cause mortality, and possibly mortality from diabetes, respiratory disease, and infectious disease. 
Common Use: Almond butter makes everything better :) Use as a spread, in dressings or sauces, or whipped up in a blender (with some dates and a pinch of salt if desired) to make instant almond milk.
Storage: to preserve freshness, keep refrigerated in a sealed container. Temperatures for long-term storage are −3 to 0 °C (27 to 32 °F) and low humidity

title: ORGANIC PEPPERCORN, pink, whole
body_html:
Botanical Name: Schinus terebinthifolius

Origin: Madagascar
Production: Pink peppercorns are not really related to peppercorns, but came to have this name because of their visual resemblance and because they also have a peppery flavor. They are actually related to cashews and mangoes.
Qualities: Pink peppercorns are light and fluffy, almost hollow, and have a citrusy flavor. Their heat and fruity aroma make them suitable for a wide variety of dishes.
Common Use: Pink peppercorns have a mildly sweet and peppery flavor. Their bold color and subtle taste make them an excellent addition to many dishes including salads, eggs, seafood and white sauces.
Storage: Store in a cool, dark, dry place in an airtight container.
Safety: This product is a member of the cashew family and may cause an allergic reaction in those with tree nut allergies. We recommend that you consult with a qualified healthcare practitioner before using any herbal products, particularly if you are pregnant, nursing, or on any medications.

title: ORGANIC PEPPERCORN, green, whole
body_html:
Botanical Name: Piper nigrum

Origin: India
Production: Piper nigrum is a flowering vine that produces berries that hang off of the plant in drupes, they start out green and eventually ripen to red. Green peppercorns are picked unripe and freeze dried to preserve the color and flavor.
Qualities: Green peppercorns, in addition to being a culinary treat, are considered high in iron, vitamin K, and antioxidants, and may have health benefits such as aiding in digestion and potential anticancer properties.
Common Use: Green peppercorns are pungent, fresh and slightly less spicy than black peppercorns. They are popular in French, Thai, and Western European cuisines.
Storage: Store in a sealed container in a cool, dark, dry place.
